front: Depois de pensar muito, resolvi chutar o balde e pedir demissão do emprego antigo.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee pehn-SAHR MWEE-too, rreh-sohl-VEE shoo-TAHR oh BAHL-jee ee peh-JEER deh-mee-SOHNG doh ehem-PREH-goo ahn-CHEE-goo.
back: After thinking a lot, I decided to throw caution to the wind and quit my old job.
context: Using 'chutar o balde' (to kick the bucket—note: does NOT mean to die in Portuguese) to mean losing patience, giving up, or making a radical, reckless decision.

front: Ele sempre quer fazer tempestade em copo d'água por qualquer problema bobo.
pronunciation: EH-lee SEHM-pree KEHR fah-ZEHR tehm-pehs-TAH-jee ehng KOH-poo DAH-gwah pohr kwahl-KEHR proh-BLEH-mah BOH-boo.
back: He always wants to make a tempest in a teapot over any silly little problem.
context: Using 'fazer tempestade em copo d'água' to describe overreacting or making a mountain out of a molehill.

front: Eu ia comprar aquela TV, mas mudei de ideia e resolvi guardar o dinheiro.
pronunciation: ew EE-ah kohm-PRAHR ah-KEH-lyah cheh-VEE, mahz moo-DAY jee ee-DAY-ah ee rreh-sohl-VEE gwahr-DAHR oh jee-NYAY-roo.
back: I was going to buy that TV, but I changed my mind and decided to save the money.
context: Using 'mudar de ideia' as the standard colloquial way to say 'to change one's mind'.

front: Ela tentou me convencer a sair, mas eu não estava muito na vibe de badalação hoje.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH mee kohn-vehn-SEHR ah sah-EER, mahz ew NOHNG ees-TAH-vah MWEE-too nah VAY-bee jee bah-dah-lah-SOHNG HOY-zhee.
back: She tried to convince me to go out, but I wasn't really in the mood for partying today.
context: Using 'estar na vibe de' (to be in the vibe/mood for) and 'badalação' (partying, clubbing, active social scene).

front: Não adianta tentar tapar o sol com a peneira; precisamos resolver a raiz do problema.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah tehn-TAHR tah-PAHR oh SOHL kohm ah peh-NAY-rah; pree-see-ZAH-mohz rreh-sohl-VEHR ah rrah-EEZ doh proh-BLEH-mah.
back: It's no use trying to cover up the sun with a sieve; we need to solve the root of the problem.
context: Using 'tapar o sol com a peneira' to describe attempting a weak, temporary cover-up for an obvious, major problem.

front: Ele é muito pão-duro; nunca quer dividir a conta do restaurante por igual.
pronunciation: EH-lee eh MWEE-too PONG-DOO-roo; NOONG-kah KEHR jee-vee-JEER ah KOHN-tah doh rrehs-taw-RHAN-chee pohr ee-GWAHL.
back: He is very cheap; he never wants to split the restaurant bill evenly.
context: Using 'pão-duro' (hard bread) to mean a stingy, cheap, or miserly person.

front: Depois do susto que levei no trânsito, resolvi colocar as barbas de molho.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z doh SOOS-too keh leh-VEE noo TRAN-zee-too, rreh-sohl-VEE koh-loh-KAHR ahz BAHR-bahz jee MOH-lyoo.
back: After the scare I had in traffic, I decided to be extremely cautious.
context: Using 'colocar as barbas de molho' (to put one's beard in soak) to mean taking precautions, playing it safe, or learning from others' mistakes.

front: Ela sempre dá um jeito de ficar em cima do muro para não chatear ninguém.
pronunciation: EH-lyah SEHM-pree DAH oong ZHAY-too jee fee-KAHR ehng SEE-mah doh MOO-roo PAH-rah NOHNG shah-cheh-AHR neeng-GEHNG.
back: She always finds a way to sit on the fence so she doesn't upset anyone.
context: Using 'ficar em cima do muro' (to stay on top of the wall) to describe being neutral, indecisive, or avoiding taking a side.

front: Eu estava cansado de escutar aquela discussão e resolvi dar de ombros.
pronunciation: ew ees-TAH-vah kahn-SAH-doh jee ees-koo-TAHR ah-KEH-lyah dees-koo-SOHNG ee rreh-sohl-VEE dahr jee OHM-broohz.
back: I was tired of listening to that argument and decided to shrug it off.
context: Using 'dar de ombros' (to give shoulders) to mean shrugging, showing indifference, or not caring.

front: Não compre esse carro sem verificar a documentação antes, para não comprar gato por lebre.
pronunciation: NOHNG KOHM-pree EHS-see KAHR-rroo sehng veh-ree-fee-KAHR ah doh-koo-mehn-tah-SOHNG AH-nteez, PAH-rah NOHNG kohm-PRAHR GAH-too pohr LEH-bree.
back: Don't buy this car without checking the documentation first, so you don't end up getting ripped off.
context: Using 'comprar gato por lebre' (to buy a cat instead of a hare) to describe being cheated, scammed, or getting a faulty product.

front: Fiquei com a pulga atrás da orelha quando ele me ofereceu aquele negócio fácil.
pronunciation: fee-KAY kohm ah POOL-gah ah-TRAHZ dah oh-REHL-yah KWAHN-doh EH-lee mee oh-feh-reh-SEW ah-KEH-lyah neh-GOH-syoo FAH-seeo.
back: I became suspicious when he offered me that easy business deal.
context: Using 'com a pulga atrás da orelha' (with a flea behind one's ear) to mean being suspicious, distrustful, or wary.

front: Ele adora contar vantagem sobre as viagens caras que faz todo ano.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah kohn-TAHR vahn-TAH-zheng SOH-bree az VYAH-zheng_Z KAH-rahz keh FAZ TOH-doh AH-noo.
back: He loves to brag about the expensive trips he takes every year.
context: Using 'contar vantagem' to mean boasting, bragging, or showing off achievements/possessions.

front: Achei que o teste seria difícil, mas na verdade foi uma uva.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY keh oh TEHS-chee seh-REE-ah jee-FEE-seeo, mahz nah vehr-DAH-jee FOY OO-mah OO-vah.
back: I thought the test would be difficult, but in reality it was a piece of cake.
context: Using 'ser uma uva' (to be a grape) or sometimes 'uma uva' as a colloquialism meaning very easy, pleasant, or high quality.

front: Ela resolveu abrir o jogo e contar toda a verdade sobre o que aconteceu na festa.
pronunciation: EH-lyah rreh-sohl-VEW ah-BREER oh ZHOH-goo ee kohn-TAHR TOH-dah ah vehr-DAH-jee SOH-bree oh keh ah-kohn-teh-SEW nah FES-tah.
back: She decided to put her cards on the table and tell the whole truth about what happened at the party.
context: Using 'abrir o jogo' (to open the game) to mean being open, frank, and revealing the truth.

front: Não dê ouvidos para aquela fofoca; as pessoas adoram falar da vida alheia.
pronunciation: NOHNG DEH oh-VEE-doohz PAH-rah ah-KEH-lyah foh-FOH-kah; az peh-SOH-ahz ah-DOH-rahng fah-LAHR dah VEE-dah ah-LYAY-ah.
back: Don't pay attention to that gossip; people love to talk about other people's lives.
context: Using 'dar ouvidos' to mean giving ear, paying attention, or believing what someone says.

front: Depois de tanta discussão, nós finalmente chegamos a um denominador comum.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TAN-tah dees-koo-SOHNG, NOHS fee-nahl-MEHN-tee sheh-GAH-mohz AH-oo deh-noh-mee-nah-DOHR koh-MOONG.
back: After so much discussion, we finally reached a common ground.
context: Using 'chegar a um denominador comum' to mean finding a compromise or reaching a mutual agreement.

front: Ele sempre tenta tirar o corpo fora quando percebe que algo vai dar errado.
pronunciation: EH-lee SEHM-pree TEHN-tah tee-RAHR oh KOHR-poo FAW-rah KWAHN-doh pehr-SEH-bee keh AW-goo VAY dahr ehr-RAH-doh.
back: He always tries to back out and dodge responsibility when he realizes something is going to go wrong.
context: Using 'tirar o corpo fora' (to pull one's body out) to mean dodging responsibility or avoiding involvement in a problematic situation.

front: Ela é muito de boa; você pode falar com ela sobre qualquer assunto sem problema.
pronunciation: EH-lyah eh MWEE-too jee BOH-ah; voh-SEH POY-jee fah-LAHR kohm EH-lyah SOH-bree kwahl-KEHR ah-SOON-too sehng proh-BLEH-mah.
back: She is very easygoing; you can talk to her about any subject without any problem.
context: Using 'de boa' to describe an easygoing, chill, or relaxed person or state of mind.

front: Ele vive pisando em ovos quando o chefe está de mau humor no escritório.
pronunciation: EH-lee VEE-vee pee-ZAHN-doh ehng OH-voohz KWAHN-doh oh SHEH-fee ees-TAH jee MAW oo-MOHR noo ees-kree-TOH-ryoo.
back: He is constantly walking on eggshells when the boss is in a bad mood in the office.
context: Using 'pisar em ovos' (to step on eggs) to mean walking on eggshells, acting with extreme caution to avoid offending someone.

front: Não leve a mal o que ele disse; ele só estava brincando.
pronunciation: NOHNG LEH-vee ah MAWL oh keh EH-lee JEE-see; EH-lee SOH ees-TAH-vah breeng-KAHN-doh.
back: Don't take offense at what he said; he was only joking.
context: Using 'levar a mal' to mean taking something badly, getting offended, or misinterpreting an action or comment.

front: Acho melhor a gente colocar as cartas na mesa para resolver essa pendência de uma vez.
pronunciation: AH-shoo meh-LYOHR ah ZHEHN-chee koh-loh-KAHR ahz KAHR-tahz nah MEH-zah PAH-rah rreh-sohl-VEHR EHS-sah pehn-DEHN-syah jee OO-mah VEH_Z.
back: I think it's better for us to put our cards on the table to resolve this outstanding issue once and for all.
context: Using 'colocar as cartas na mesa' to mean being completely transparent, honest, and direct about a situation.

front: Ela tentou dar uma de migué fingindo que não sabia do prazo do relatório.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH dahr OO-mah jee mee-GHEH feeng-ZHEHN-doh keh NOHNG sah-BEE-ah doh PRAH-zoo doh rreh-lah-TOH-ryoo.
back: She tried to play dumb by pretending she didn't know about the report's deadline.
context: Using 'dar uma de migué' or 'dar migué' to mean playing dumb, making a weak excuse, or slacking off.

front: Se você continuar agindo com essa teimosia, vai acabar dando com a cara na parede.
pronunciation: see voh-SEH kohn-chee-NWAHR ah-ZHEENG-doh kohm EHS-sah tay-moh-ZEE-ah, VAY ah-kah-BAHR DAHN-doh kohm ah KAH-rah nah pah-REH-jee.
back: If you keep acting with that stubbornness, you will end up hitting a brick wall.
context: Using 'dar com a cara na parede' or 'dar com a cara na porta' to mean failing, hitting a dead end, or being rejected/frustrated.

front: Ele sempre fala que vai começar a correr, mas no final das contas, fica só no gogó.
pronunciation: EH-lee SEHM-pree FAH-lyah keh VAY koh-meh-SAHR ah kohr-REHR, mahz noo fee-NAHL daz KOHN-tahz, FEE-kah SOH noo goh-GOH.
back: He always says he is going to start running, but at the end of the day, it's all talk.
context: Using 'ficar só no gogó' (to stay only in the larynx/throat) to mean all talk and no action.

front: Não adianta chorar sobre o leite derramado; o importante agora é focar na solução.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah sho-RAHR SOH-bree oh LAY-chee dehr-rah-MAH-doh; oh eem-pohr-TAHN-chee ah-GOH-rah eh foh-KAHR nah soh-loo-SOHNG.
back: It's no use crying over spilled milk; the important thing now is to focus on the solution.
context: The Brazilian equivalent of 'crying over spilled milk' using identical imagery ('chorar sobre o leite derramado').

front: Ela resolveu chutar o pau da barraca e falou tudo o que pensava do chefe.
pronunciation: EH-lyah rreh-sohl-VEW shoo-TAHR oh PAW dah bahr-RAH-kah ee fah-LOH TOO-doh oh keh pehn-SAH-vah doh SHEH-fee.
back: She decided to kick up a row and said everything she thought about the boss.
context: Using 'chutar o pau da barraca' (to kick the tent pole) to mean losing control, abandoning diplomacy, or causing a massive confrontation.

front: Ele adora colher os frutos do trabalho alheio sem fazer o menor esforço.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah koh-LYEHR ooz FROO-toohz doh trah-BAH-lyoo ah-LYAY-oo sehng fah-ZEHR oh meh-NYOHR ees-FOHR-soo.
back: He loves to reap the benefits of other people's work without making the slightest effort.
context: Using 'colher os frutos' (to reap the fruits/benefits) of someone else's labor ('trabalho alheio').

front: Estou cansado de andar em círculos e não chegar a lugar nenhum com esse plano.
pronunciation: ees-TOY kahn-SAH-doh jee ahn-DAHR ehng SEER-koo-loohz ee NOHNG sheh-GAHR ah loo-GAHR neheng-GOONG kohm EHS-see PLAH-noo.
back: I am tired of running in circles and getting nowhere with this plan.
context: Using 'andar em círculos' (to walk in circles) to describe lack of progress or repetitive, fruitless action.

front: Ela tentou dar uma de espertinha na fila, mas todo mundo reclamou na hora.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH dahr OO-mah jee ehs-pehr-CHEENG-yah nah FEE-lyah, mahz TOH-doh MOON-doo rreh-klah-MOH nah OH-rah.
back: She tried to act clever and cut the line, but everyone complained immediately.
context: Using 'dar uma de espertinha' to mean trying to act slyly, pull a fast one, or bypass rules selfishly.

front: Acho melhor você maneirar nas críticas para não arrumar sarna para se coçar.
pronunciation: AH-shoo meh-LYOHR voh-SEH mah-nay-RAHR naz KREE-chee-kahz PAH-rah NOHNG ah-rroo-MAHR SAHR-nah PAH-rah see koh-SAHR.
back: I think you'd better tone down the criticism so you don't look for unnecessary trouble.
context: Using 'arrumar sarna para se coçar' (to get scabies to scratch) to mean looking for trouble or bringing hassle upon oneself.

front: Ele é um cara cabeça-dura; nunca aceita opiniões diferentes da dele.
pronunciation: EH-lee eh oong KAH-rah kah-BEH-sah DOO-rah; NOONG-kah ah-SAY-tah oh-pee-NYONG_Z jee-feh-REHN-cheez dah DEH-lee.
back: He is a stubborn guy; he never accepts opinions different from his own.
context: Using 'cabeça-dura' (hard-headed) as a very common colloquial term for stubborn.

front: A viagem foi cancelada, mas nós conseguimos dar a volta por cima e aproveitar o feriado em casa.
pronunciation: ah VYAH-zheng FOY kahn-seh-LAH-dah, mahz NOHS kohn-seh-GHEE-mohz dahr ah VOHL-tah pohr SEE-mah ee ah-proh-vay-TAHR oh feh-ree-AH-doh ehng KAH-zah.
back: The trip was canceled, but we managed to bounce back and enjoy the holiday at home.
context: Using 'dar a volta por cima' to mean bouncing back, turning a bad situation around, or overcoming a setback.

front: Ele tentou passar a perna no irmão na divisão dos bens, mas a justiça agiu rápido.
pronunciation: EH-lee tehn-TOH pah-SAHR ah PEHR-nah noo eer-MONG nah jee-vee-ZOHNG doohz BEHNG_Z, mahz ah zhoos-CHEE-sah ah-ZHEW RRAH-pee-doh.
back: He tried to swindle his brother in the division of assets, but justice acted quickly.
context: Using 'passar a perna' (to slip a leg past someone) to mean cheating, swindling, or taking unfair advantage of someone.

front: Não compre aquela marca; é famosa por vender gato por lebre para clientes desatentos.
pronunciation: NOHNG KOHM-pree ah-KEH-lyah MAHR-kah; eh fah-MOH-zah pohr vehn-DEHR GAH-too pohr LEH-bree PAH-rah klee-EHN-cheez dez-ah-TEHN-toohz.
back: Don't buy that brand; it is famous for ripping off inattentive customers.
context: Another application of 'vender gato por lebre', emphasizing a scam or bait-and-switch scheme.

front: Depois de muito esforço, finalmente consegui colocar a casa em ordem.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee MWEE-too ees-FOHR-soo, fee-nahl-MEHN-tee kohn-seh-GWEE koh-loh-KAHR ah KAH-sah ehng OHR-deheng.
back: After a lot of effort, I finally managed to get things in order.
context: Using 'colocar a casa em ordem' (to put the house in order) to mean organizing one's life, affairs, or physical space.

front: Ela adora dar pitaco na decoração da minha sala, mesmo sem eu pedir nada.
pronunciation: EH-lyah ah-DOH-rah dahr pee-TAH-koo nah deh-koh-rah-SOHNG dah MEENG-yah SAH-lyah, MEHS-moo sehng ew peh-JEER NAH-dah.
back: She loves to give unsolicited opinions on my living room decor, even without me asking for anything.
context: Using 'dar pitaco' to mean giving unsolicited advice, opinions, or meddling in someone else's business.

front: Achei que ia ser difícil convencer meus pais, mas foi mel na chupeta.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY keh EE-ah SEHR jee-FEE-seeo kohn-vehn-SEHR MEW_Z PY_Z, mahz FOY MEHL nah shoo-PEH-tah.
back: I thought it would be difficult to convince my parents, but it was a walk in the park.
context: Using 'mel na chupeta' (honey on the pacifier) to mean something exceptionally easy, effortless, or pleasant.

front: Se você não focar nos estudos agora, vai acabar comendo mosca e perdendo a vaga.
pronunciation: see voh-SEH NOHNG foh-KAHR nooz ees-TOO-doohz ah-GOH-rah, VAY ah-kah-BAHR koh-MEHND-do MOHS-kah ee pehr-DEHN-doh ah VAH-gah.
back: If you don't focus on your studies now, you will end up missing out and losing the spot.
context: Using 'comer mosca' (to eat a fly) to mean missing an opportunity, being careless, or spacing out and failing to seize a moment.

front: Ele quis bancar o espertalhão na negociação, mas acabou saindo no prejuízo.
pronunciation: EH-lee keez bahng-KAHR oh ehes-pehr-tah-LYONG nah neh-goh-sya-SOHNG, mahz ah-kah-BOH sah-EENG-doh noo preh-zhoo-EE-zoo.
back: He wanted to play the smart-aleck in the negotiation, but ended up taking a financial hit.
context: Using 'bancar o espertalhão' to mean acting like a know-it-all or attempting to outsmart others, often with negative consequences.

front: A palestra estava tão chata que tive que fazer um esforço enorme para não cochilar.
pronunciation: ah pah-LEHS-trah ees-TAH-vah TOHNG SHAH-tah keh CHEE-vee keh fah-ZEHR oong ees-FOHR-soo ee-NOHR-mee PAH-rah NOHNG koh-shee-LAHR.
back: The lecture was so boring that I had to make a huge effort not to doze off.
context: Using 'cochilar' to mean taking a nap, dozing off, or losing focus momentarily.

front: Depois de perder o voo, ela ficou com uma cara de tacho no aeroporto.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee pehr-DEHR oh VOH-oo, EH-lyah fee-KOH kohm OO-mah KAH-rah jee TAH-shoo noo ah-eh-roh-POHR-too.
back: After missing the flight, she was left with an embarrassed and foolish look on her face at the airport.
context: Using 'ficar com cara de tacho' (to have a face like a copper pan) to describe a look of sheepishness, embarrassment, or disappointment after a failure.

front: A reunião fluiu muito bem porque todos decidiram jogar limpo desde o início.
pronunciation: ah rrehoo-nee-ONG floo-EW MWEE-too BEHNG pohr-KEH TOH-doohz jee-see-JEE-roong zho-GAHR LEEM-poo DEHS-jee oh ee-NEE-syoo.
back: The meeting flowed really well because everyone decided to play fair from the start.
context: Using 'jogar limpo' (to play clean) to mean acting with transparency, honesty, and fair play.

front: Não aguento mais esse diz-que-me-diz no escritório; prefiro focar no meu trabalho.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-GWEHN-too MYS EHS-see JEE_Z-keh-mee-JEE_Z noo ees-kree-TOH-ryoo; preh-FEE-roo foh-KAHR noo MEW trah-BAH-lyoo.
back: I can't stand this hearsay and gossip in the office anymore; I prefer to focus on my work.
context: Using 'diz-que-me-diz' or 'diz-que-me-disse' (he-says-she-says) to describe petty office gossip, rumors, or hearsay.

front: Ele resolveu abrir mão do cargo de gerência para passar mais tempo com a família.
pronunciation: EH-lee rreh-sohl-VEW ah-BREER MONG doh KAHR-goo jee zheh-REHN-syah PAH-rah pah-SAHR MYS TEHM-poo kohm ah fah-MEE-lyah.
back: He decided to give up his managerial position to spend more time with his family.
context: Using 'abrir mão' (to open hand) to mean relinquishing, sacrificing, or giving up a privilege or possession.

front: Achei que ele ia me apoiar, mas ele me deixou na mão no momento mais difícil.
pronunciation: ah-SHAY keh EH-lee EE-ah mee ah-poy-AHR, mahz EH-lee mee day-SHOH nah MONG noo moh-MEHN-too MYS jee-FEE-seeo.
back: I thought he was going to support me, but he left me in the lurch at the most difficult moment.
context: Using 'deixar na mão' (to leave in the hand) to mean letting someone down, leaving them stranded, or failing to help when needed.
